name: Live Biotherapeutics and Engineered Microbes Surveillance for June 6, 2026
summary: Surveillance bundle adding a new Pediococcus acidilactici PA53 older-adult well-being and gut-health RCT while keeping the track at low-confidence human_functional_benefit with no LEV-relevant timing upgrade.

scope.question: What changed for Live Biotherapeutics and Engineered Microbes since the June 1, 2026 public baseline publication, and does any change justify a public update?

proposed_changes.summary: Add PA53 older-adult well-being RCT source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This is a post-baseline randomized older-adult human trial directly relevant to the live-biotherapeutic functional evidence boundary.

proposed_changes.summary: Normalize PA53 as a strain-defined live microbial intervention.
proposed_changes.rationale: The new source uses a product-specific Pediococcus strain that should not be collapsed into generic probiotics.

proposed_changes.summary: Add PA53 older-adult RCT study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record captures the population, design, endpoint classes, intervention, and source links needed for the new finding.

proposed_changes.summary: Add bounded older-adult PA53 well-being, gut-health, sleep, cytokine, and microbiota finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding updates the support map while preserving short-duration, subjective-endpoint, no-durability, and taxonomy-overlap caveats.

proposed_changes.summary: Refresh outlook support map for the PA53 surveillance delta.
proposed_changes.rationale: The existing outlook says the human functional signal is mainly TWK10; the new PA53 RCT should be reflected without increasing confidence, stage, momentum, or LEV-relevant timing.

proposed_outlook_implications.note: Add PA53 as a second bounded older-adult human signal while keeping evidence_stage human_functional_benefit, momentum accelerating, confidence low, and no LEV timing upgrade.

next_actions: Publication complete. Review downstream pages if this change affects shared surfaces.
